prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. Starting on July 31, 2022, an escalation of friction between Serbia and Kosovo took place, stemming from the fact that the eleven-year term of validity for vehicle documents, which was established on August 1, 2022, between the Kosovan government and ethnic Serbs residing in North Kosovo, had come to an end. Kosovo, having declared its sovereignty in 2008, entered into an accord with Serbia in 2011, which addressed the usage of vehicle registration plates within North Kosovo. This particular arrangement was intended to facilitate a transition from Serbian-issued license plates to those considered neutral. The aforementioned agreement was subsequently prolonged in 2016, ultimately expiring in 2021, leading to a period of instability that concluded with a new understanding, lifting the prohibition on license plates issued by Kosovo within Serbia. Following the declaration that Serbian nationals entering Kosovo would be issued with specific entry and exit paperwork.
B. Beginning on 31 July 2022, tensions between Serbia and Kosovo heightened due to the expiration of the eleven-year validity period of documents for cars on 1 August 2022, between the government of Kosovo and the Serbs in North Kosovo. Kosovo, which declared independence in 2008, signed an agreement with Serbia in 2011 that concluded on the use of license plates in North Kosovo. This agreement was supposed to change license plates from the ones that were issued by Serbia to neutral ones. The agreement was extended in 2016 and it expired in 2021 after which a crisis occurred and it ended with an agreement that ended the ban of Kosovo-issued license plates in Serbia. After the announcement that Serbian citizens who enter Kosovo will receive entry and exit
C. The escalation of tensions between Serbia and Kosovo began on 31 July 2022 when the eleven-year validity period of documents for cars expired on 1 August 2022, between the government of Kosovo and the Serbs in North Kosovo. Kosovo, which declared independence in 2011, signed an agreement with Serbia in 2011 that aimed to change license plates from Serbian ones to neutral ones. The agreement was later extended in 2016 and expired in 2021. A crisis ensued after an agreement was reached that ended the ban of Kosovo-issued license plates in Serbia.

Answer:

options.A: Starting on July 31, 2022, an escalation of friction between Serbia and Kosovo took place, stemming from the fact that the eleven-year term of validity for vehicle documents, which was established on August 1, 2022, between the Kosovan government and ethnic Serbs residing in North Kosovo, had come to an end. Kosovo, having declared its sovereignty in 2008, entered into an accord with Serbia in 2011, which addressed the usage of vehicle registration plates within North Kosovo. This particular arrangement was intended to facilitate a transition from Serbian-issued license plates to those considered neutral. The aforementioned agreement was subsequently prolonged in 2016, ultimately expiring in 2021, leading to a period of instability that concluded with a new understanding, lifting the prohibition on license plates issued by Kosovo within Serbia. Following the declaration that Serbian nationals entering Kosovo would be issued with specific entry and exit paperwork.
options.B: Beginning on 31 July 2022, tensions between Serbia and Kosovo heightened due to the expiration of the eleven-year validity period of documents for cars on 1 August 2022, between the government of Kosovo and the Serbs in North Kosovo. Kosovo, which declared independence in 2008, signed an agreement with Serbia in 2011 that concluded on the use of license plates in North Kosovo. This agreement was supposed to change license plates from the ones that were issued by Serbia to neutral ones. The agreement was extended in 2016 and it expired in 2021 after which a crisis occurred and it ended with an agreement that ended the ban of Kosovo-issued license plates in Serbia. options.C: The escalation of tensions between Serbia and Kosovo began on 31 July 2022 when the eleven-year validity period of documents for cars expired on 1 August 2022, between the government of Kosovo and the Serbs in North Kosovo. Kosovo, which declared independence in 2011, signed an agreement with Serbia in 2011 that aimed to change license plates from Serbian ones to neutral ones. The agreement was later extended in 2016 and expired in 2021. A crisis ensued after an agreement was reached that ended the ban of Kosovo-issued license plates in Serbia.
